sections.heading: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co-
sections.summary: The Information Commissioner may provide information and assistance to, and cooperate with, public authorities in the EU if it determines that such cooperation would benefit effective supervision of digital service providers (both within and outside the UK), particularly in response to incidents notified under regulation 12(3). This section outlines the Information Commissioner's authority to conduct inspections on behalf of competent authorities, requiring the OES or RDSP to pay reasonable costs, cooperate with inspectors, provide access to premises, allow examination of documents and equipment, and comply with inspector requests. Inspectors may enter premises, secure relevant materials, take statements, conduct tests, and ensure materials are kept secure. The inspector must produce identification and maintain security of removed materials. This section outlines the procedural requirements for serving enforcement notices under the Information Commissioner or relevant competent authority. It mandates that before issuing an enforcement notice, the authority must inform the OES (Organisation for Electronic Services) or RDSP (Regulated Data Service Provider) of the alleged failure and
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes inspection procedures that may impact data transfer by requiring access to systems and data during audits, potentially affecting cross-border data handling if the OES/RDSP is involved in international data flows. This section establishes the procedural safeguards for enforcement actions, requiring prior notification to OES/RDSPs and consideration of representations before imposing penalties. It directly impacts DTIA by defining the process for addressing non-compliance with data protection obligations across borders. This section is relevant for DTIAs as it establishes

requirements.text: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise cooperate with, a public authority in the EU if the Information Commissioner considers that to do so would be in the interests of effective supervision of digital service providers (whether inside or outside the United Kingdom), including in the event of an incident notified under regulation 12(3).
The OES or RDSP must pay the reasonable costs of the inspection if required by the relevant competent authority or Information Commissioner
The OES or RDSP must co-operate with the inspector
The OES or RDSP must provide the inspector with access to their premises in accordance with paragraph (5)(a)
The OES or RDSP must allow the inspector to examine, print, copy or remove any document or information, and examine or remove any material or equipment in accordance with paragraph (5)(d)
The OES or RDSP must allow the inspector access to any person from whom the inspector seeks relevant information for the purposes of the inspection
The OES or RDSP must not intentionally obstruct an inspector performing their functions
The OES or RDSP must comply with any request made by, or requirement of, an inspector performing their functions
The inspector may enter premises of an OES or RDSP at any reasonable time if the inspector has reasonable grounds to believe entry may be necessary for the inspection
The OES or RDSP must leave undisturbed and not dispose of, render inaccessible or alter relevant material, documents, information or equipment for the period necessary for the inspection
The OES or RDSP must produce and provide the inspector with access to relevant material, documents, information or equipment either immediately or within a specified period
The inspector must produce proof of identity if requested by any person at the premises
The inspector must take appropriate and proportionate measures to ensure removed materials are kept secure from unauthorised access, interference and physical damage
Before serving an enforcement notice, the relevant competent authority or Information Commissioner must inform the OES or RDSP of the alleged failure and how and by when representations may be made.
The relevant competent authority or Information Commissioner may serve an enforcement notice on the OES or RDSP within a reasonable time after informing them of the alleged failure.
The relevant competent authority or Information Commissioner must have regard to any representations made under paragraph (2A)(b).
The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co-operate with, designated competent authorities for the OES and RDSP.

The Information Commissioner may treat certain court judgments as if they were judgments entered in the county court for enforcement purposes in England and Wales, and similarly for Northern Ireland under specific orders.

For the essential service of electricity supply in Great Britain, electricity undertakings that supply to more than 250,000 final customers or have a generation capacity of 2 gigawatts or more (including affiliated undertakings) are subject to threshold requirements.

For the essential service of electricity supply in Northern Ireland, holders of supply licences supplying to more than 8,000 consumers or with a generating capacity of 350 megawatts or more are subject to threshold requirements.
For the essential service of the single electricity market in Northern Ireland, holders of Single Electricity Market operator licences are subject to threshold requirements.

For the essential service of electricity transmission in Great Britain, transmission system operators with potential to disrupt delivery to more than 250,000 final customers or holders of offshore transmission licences with cumulative capacity of 2 gigawatts or more are subject to threshold requirements.

For the essential service of electricity distribution in Great Britain, distribution system operators with potential to disrupt delivery to more than 250,000 final customers are subject to threshold requirements.
For the essential service of oil transmission by pipeline in Great Britain, operators with throughput of more than 500,000 tonnes of crude oil per year are subject to threshold requirements.
For the essential service of oil transmission by pipeline in Northern Ireland, operators with throughput of more than 50,000 tonnes of crude oil per year are subject to threshold requirements.
For the essential service of oil processing facilities, operators with throughput of more than 3,000,000 tonnes of oil equivalent per year are subject to threshold requirements.

For the essential service of crude oil based fuel production, refining, storage, and transmission in Great Britain, operators with capacity exceeding 500,000 tonnes of crude oil based fuel in storage, production, or supply are subject to threshold requirements.

For the essential service of crude oil based fuel production, refining, storage, and transmission in Northern Ireland, operators with storage capacity exceeding 50,000 tonnes of crude oil based fuel are subject to threshold requirements.
has the meaning given by section 90(1) of the Energy Act 2011
gas processing facility has the meaning given by section 12(6) of the Gas Act 1995
gas processing operation means any of the following operations—

oil equivalent means petroleum and, for the purposes of assessments of throughput, where petroleum is in a gaseous state 1,100 cubic meters of this petroleum at a temperature of 15 degrees Celsius and pressure of one atmosphere is counted as equivalent to one tonne
oil processing facility means any facility which carries out oil processing operations
oil processing operations means any of the following operations—
operator means—
petroleum has the same meaning as in section 1 of the Petroleum Act 1998
petroleum production project means a project carried out by virtue of a licence granted under—
piped gas means gas which—
pipeline means a pipe or system of pipes for the conveyance of anything
production installation has the meaning given by regulation 2(1) of the Offshore Installations (Safety Case) Regulations 2005

relevant offshore installation means an offshore installation within the meaning of section 44 of the Petroleum Act 1998 which carries on the activities mentioned in subsection (3)(a) or (c) of that section and is a relevant offshore installation only to the extent it is used to carry on those activities
terminal includes—
upstream petroleum pipeline means a pipeline or one of a network of pipelines which is—
For the essential service of gas supply, supply undertakings that supply gas to more than 250,000 final customers in Great Britain

For the essential service of gas supply, the holder of a supply licence under Article 8(1)(c) of the Gas (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 who supplies gas to more than 2,000 customers in Northern Ireland
For the essential service of gas transmission, transmission system operators with a potential to disrupt delivery to more than 250,000 final customers in Great Britain

For the essential service of gas transmission, holders of interconnector licences where the gas interconnector has the technological capacity to input more than 20 million cubic metres of gas per day to a transmission system
For the essential service of gas transmission in Northern Ireland, the holder of a gas conveyance licence under Article 8(1)(a) of the Gas (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
For the essential service of gas distribution, distribution system operators with a potential to disrupt delivery to more than 250,000 final customers in Great Britain
For the essential service of gas distribution in Northern Ireland, the holder of a licence under Article 8(1)(a) of the Gas (Northern Ireland) Order 1996

For the essential service of gas storage facilities, storage system operators where the storage facility has the technological capacity to input more than 20 million cubic metres of gas per day to a transmission system in Great Britain
For the essential service of gas storage facilities in Northern Ireland, the holder of a licence under Article 8(1)(b) of the Gas (Northern Ireland) Order 1996

For the essential service of LNG facilities, LNG system operators where the LNG facility has the technological capacity to input more than 20 million cubic metres of gas per day to a transmission system in Great Britain
For the essential service of LNG facilities in Northern Ireland, the holder of a licence under Article 8(1)(d) of the Gas (Northern Ireland) Order 1996
For the essential service of relevant gas processing facilities, operators of facilities with a throughput of more than 3,000,000 tonnes of oil equivalent per year
For the essential service of petroleum production projects (other than gas storage), operators of installations or pipelines with a throughput of more than 3,000,000 tonnes of oil equivalent per year
An operator of vessel traffic services at a port which has annual passenger numbers greater than 10 million

An operator of vessel traffic services at a port which fulfils at least one of the following criteria—(i) it handles more than fifteen percent of the total roll-on roll-off traffic in the United Kingdom; (ii) it handles more than fifteen percent of the total lift-on lift-off traffic in the United Kingdom; (iii) it handles more than ten percent of the total liquid bulk traffic in the United Kingdom; or (iv) it handles more than twenty percent of the total biomass fuel traffic in the United Kingdom

sections.source_quote: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co-operate with, a public authority in the EU if the Information Commissioner considers that to do so would be in the interests of effective supervision of digital service providers (whether inside or outside the United Kingdom), including in the event of an incident notified under regulation 12(3).

sections.heading: The threshold requirement which applies to the essential service of the supply of potable water
sections.summary: This section defines the threshold requirement for the essential service of potable water supply in the UK, specifying that it applies when water is supplied to 200,000 or more people. The threshold is a quantitative measure used to determine whether a water service qualifies as an essential service under the regulations.

requirements.text: The threshold requirement for the essential service of the supply of potable water in the United Kingdom is the supply of water to 200,000 or more people.

sections.source_quote: 9. The threshold requirement which applies to the essential service of the supply of potable water in the United Kingdom is the supply of water to 200,000 or more people.
